reasoning: The informal statement "the center of the product of two groups is the product of their centers" asserts that Z(G × H) ≅ Z(G) × Z(H) for arbitrary groups G and H. The Lean statement `Nonempty (center (G × H) ≃* (center G) × (center H))` expresses precisely this as the existence of a group isomorphism (multiplicative equivalence). Because `center (G × H)` (a subtype of G × H) and `center G × center H` (a product of subtypes) are genuinely different Lean types, an isomorphism is the natural way to encode the informal "is". The quantification matches: both G and H are arbitrary groups (`{G H : Type*} [Group G] [Group H]`). No extra hypotheses (finiteness, abelianness, decidability, etc.) are added or dropped. This is a standard, faithful Mathlib-style encoding of this classical Artin exercise.
